title: Build indexes CONCURRENTLY in production
description: On a populated, live table, create and drop indexes with CONCURRENTLY so writers are not blocked.
body: Use when adding or removing an index on a table that takes live traffic. Do not run a plain CREATE INDEX on a large hot table; it takes a SHARE lock that blocks writes for the whole build. Actions: use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (and DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY), run it outside a transaction block, and check for and clean up an INVALID index if the build fails partway. Validation signals include writes continuing during the build and pg_index showing indisvalid true afterward. Risk boundary: a non-concurrent index build on a production hot table is an availability incident; if it must run plain, schedule a maintenance window and get a human gate.
